title: Onboarding — COMPANION complete flow: 3 slides, Back hidden on slide 1, Get Started on last slide, navigate to companion home

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
A verified COMPANION account exists and credentials are available (DATA-1, DATA-2).
The COMPANION onboarding screen is reachable. Note: /(companion)/_layout.tsx does NOT have an onboarding guard in M02. Navigate directly to the screen.

journey.description: Open login screen with fresh context.
Wait for login to complete. COMPANION home is the destination.
Navigate directly to COMPANION onboarding since /(companion)/_layout.tsx does not have an onboarding guard in M02. This simulates a new COMPANION user first encounter.
Tap Get Started on the last slide.
markOnboardingComplete() is called, then router.replace to /(companion)/home.

assertions.description: Back button is NOT visible on slide 1.

target.text: Your trusted companion experience starts here.

assertions.description: Slide 2 COMPANION-specific title visible — differs from CLIENT onboarding.
Back button IS visible on slide 2.
Button label is 'Get Started' on the last slide.

target.text: Complete your profile and start accepting bookings.

assertions.description: After Get Started, navigates to COMPANION home.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: Unlike the CLIENT onboarding test, COMPANION onboarding is navigated to directly (step 6) because the /(companion)/_layout.tsx does not guard onboarding in M02.
The COMPANION slide content differs from CLIENT: slide 2 is 'Be a Great Companion' and slide 3 body is 'Complete your profile and start accepting bookings.'

The onboarding re-open skip assertion is NOT included in this test because /(companion)/_layout.tsx does not have the onboarding guard. If a guard is added in a future milestone, this test should be updated.
If the session expires between login (step 4) and goToRoute (step 6), the app may redirect to login. The validator should ensure the session token is valid.
